questions.a: A fixed-size block that stores sorted keys and pointers.

questions.q: Why are B-trees strong for reads and range scans?
questions.a: Their balanced shape and sorted pages let the engine find nearby keys with few I/Os.

questions.q: When would you choose a B-tree engine over an LSM-tree engine?
questions.a: When point lookups and range queries matter more than write throughput.
An in-memory write buffer flushed later as immutable sorted files.

questions.q: Why are LSM-trees good for write-heavy workloads?
questions.a: Writes become sequential appends, which are much cheaper than random page updates.

questions.q: When would you choose an LSM-tree over a B-tree?
questions.a: When ingest rate is high and you can tolerate compaction work for faster writes.
Crash recovery and durability for committed changes.

questions.q: Why must a change hit the WAL before data pages?
questions.a: If the database crashes, it can replay the log and recover committed work.

questions.q: What happens if checkpoints are too infrequent?
questions.a: Recovery gets slower and the log grows larger because more changes remain to replay.
A memory cache for database pages.
They have changed in memory but have not yet been flushed to disk.

questions.q: When do buffer pool misses become a major problem?
questions.a: When the working set is larger than memory and the engine keeps reading cold pages from disk.

questions.q: What does a cost-based optimizer do?
questions.a: It picks the plan with the lowest estimated cost.

questions.q: Why does ANALYZE or statistics collection matter?
questions.a: The optimizer needs row counts, cardinalities, and distributions to estimate a good plan.

questions.q: How can bad cardinality estimates hurt a query?
questions.a: They can cause the engine to choose the wrong join order, scan type, or index.

questions.q: Why does VACUUM exist in systems like Postgres?
questions.a: It reclaims dead row versions and prevents tables from filling with obsolete data.

questions.q: When can a low-cardinality index hurt performance?
questions.a: When the index is not selective enough and the engine still has to read most of the table.
